author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg:
additional procedure to extract 1,000 records from the manage.unitynodes.io page (after the webpage update it extracts only the first 20)

1. Open DevTools (F12) → Go to Network tab.
2. Reload the page and find the request named rewards_get_allocations.
3. Right-click on that request row → Copy → Copy as fetch.
4. Go to Console tab.
5. Paste the code.
6. If Chrome says “Paste is not allowed”, type allow pasting, press Enter and paste the text again.
7. Make these two changes only:
    Change "take": 20 → "take": 1000
    Change "credentials": "include" → "credentials": "same-origin"

8. Press Enter.
9. Wait a few seconds → Move back to Network tab and copy the results that include 1,000 records from the second rewards_get_allocations

author: Sojourner
msg: I started with 7 WiFi only android phones on a mini farm at home. I converted 1 to a sim only phone, which is currently my top earner. My family and I all use iPhones and have brought them all online since iPhone app was accepted. Now I have 14 phones on the network (added an old iPhone and old iPad to the WiFi only phones) 5 of which are sim but connect to WiFi when at home. All of those phones are getting the same or less earnings as my WiFi only Android phones except for 1, which is my sons phone at college. It definitely appears that there is a hard cap per IP above a certain level. I’m shutting down 6 WiFi only phones to see if these sim iPhones will increase in earnings. We’ll see.

author: Quoia75
msg:
@unityassistant

🌟I had an opportunity to log into the new version of the Web Management site today. Here are a few requested updates:


💰• **Bank Account Registration** – please add more countries for Latin America. Some big ones are missing like Mexico, Argentina, Columbia, Peru, Panama, Chile, Bolivia, and Venezuela.


🧰• **Folder Segmentation** – Allow the creation of folders for easier management of licenses. Case example: creating different splits and lease terms for different groups of ULOs. 

How this could work – when you select the licenses you want to segment, an option can appear beneath “REVOKE LICENSES” labeled “ADD TO FOLDER”. In the next screen, the UNO will then select which folder they would like to use, or be prompted to create a new one. Existing folders would live under a new tab in the left-hand side bar and the folders should also show the number of licenses each contains.


**🛠• Add Node Management** – For UNOs with multiple nodes, it would be wonderful to be able to see these individual nodes within the web GUI and to be able to easily move licenses between nodes via "Bulk Changes". I’m not certain if __uno.unitynodes.io__ is still relevant or if your team is planning to sunset it (I recommend sunsetting and concentrating effort on making the Management site as great as possible).


**🏷• Improve Filter Experience** – If an UNO applies a filter and then takes action on one of the filtered assets, the filter is removed immediately. This requires the UNO to then apply the filter again and again, say for each time they want to revoke a license. __Sometimes examining an individual license instead of doing a bulk revoke action makes more sense__. 

Having to reapply the filter multiple times becomes cumbersome after a while. Better UX: keep the filter in place until the UNO decides to remove it or the session ends, or set it on a 24 hour timer. If no more assets match the filter, just show a messages saying so.


**☝️• A Really Nice to have addition** – It would be great if we could set a 3-5 character personal pre-fix for our licenses, for example: 

     **KATS**-0x010e……..
     **DAWGS**-0x010e……..

Etc.

This would make it easier for ULOs to find our specific licenses in the Marketplace. It could be added under SETTINGS and the prefix could be set PER NODE.


**THANK YOU🙏 **– The CSV Export tool works flawlessly. Significant upgrade making life so so much easier.🎉💯


I think the web management portal is a solid work-in-progress and I’m looking forward to seeing it get even better over the upcoming weeks. 🔥🔥🔥

@unityassistant

One more thing noticed with the Management website's filtering logic: Once a license has a private lease code generated it no longer shows under the "LEASED" or "NOT LEASED" filters. 


Request: Can we have filters created that relate specifically to generated leases? Example:


**LEASE CREATED
BOUND
NOT BOUND**


Additionally, these three filters should be able to be combined for more intuitive filtering. This would be incredibly helpful.🙌💯

author: Quoia75
msg:
This is will be vital to know.

Will auto-revoke actually be applied to generated lease codes? 
How will that effect people keeping them inventoried in CSVs or Databases?

It probably will make more sense to keep auto-revoked tied to poor uptime and connectivity for licenses that were bound but for whatever reason, stopped producing.

For people managing a collection of generate codes, they can use various code-based approaches in their own setups to re-issue unused leases as long as Unity (the company) is not auto revoking them for not being used.

Another approach to auto-revoke is just to make it something that an UNO has the option to toggle on or off (preferred ✔️)

author: LE
msg:
Checked everything that is mentioned surrounding this issue. 

iOS check list:

1. When first connecting, leave the app open for about 20 minutes.
2. Do not swipe the app away. Always have in open in the background.
3. Go to the Settings > General > Background App Refresh and ensure it is enabled for Unity.
4. Try to not enable Low Power save mode as it can interfere. 
5. Enable notifications in both device settings + inside the app.
6. Go to the Settings > Screen Time > add Unity to the list of "‘Always Allowed".

author: Page Øne
msg:
Perplexity: 

Strategic Takeaway
Unity Node fills a gap that Chainlink never tried to fill: the decentralized verification of physical telecommunications infrastructure. While Chainlink has become the standard oracle layer for connecting blockchains to financial markets and enterprise data, Unity Node is building the equivalent layer for the $2 trillion global telecom industry — one that monetizes real carrier demand for network integrity rather than DeFi demand for price data. They are complementary technologies at different layers, not direct substitutes. A project needing DeFi price feeds would use Chainlink; a telecom carrier needing decentralized network auditing would use Unity.
